Hello and welcome to today's workshop, selling with story, the brand boosting blueprint that turns your customers into heroes and clicks into dollars with Donald Miller. Guys, today is all about storytelling for your brand. So you can stop losing leads and customers and start hooking them with your incredible story that puts them in the hero or heroine's boots. This method, the story brand method, is the formula that screenwriters use to create blockbusters that captivate audiences. And today, story brand founder and creator Donald Miller is going to take you step by step through crafting messaging that really resonates with your customers. And then he's going to show you how to leverage that script in every asset throughout your funnel. And today, you will get a brand script with the in-depth customer messaging to use throughout your funnel, a wire-framed home page with the messaging that we talked about, and a completed workbook with your story brand. So you have everything you need to share with your team and start implementing as soon as you're ready. That's going to include talking points, a remodeled funnel, and a marketing checklist to keep everything super organized. And before we leave today, you will also get the chance to have Donald Miller review your landing pages live in a virtual hot seat. So before we get started, we have a couple housekeeping things. As you can see, we are now using Zoom. I think it's going to be way more fun this way, but we have to work together to make sure that we can pull this off. So guys, check now and make sure that you're on mute. We need you guys to stay muted at all times because we're all here to hear from Donald Miller and not a coffee grinder or a peloton in the background. But keep your video on as much as you can. We can see you and Donald is excited to present to real humans and not a sea of black boxes. And a little tip, go ahead and pin selling with story or keep the Zoom in your speaker view so that you're not losing sight of the presentation. This is important, guys. If you have a question for Donald, make sure to use the Q&A feature at the bottom of your screen. Jenna is our chat moderator for today, so she's going to make sure that we get those questions to Donald on stage. That chat itself is a great place to keep the conversation going, share big takeaways, and ask questions like, wait, what did that last slide say again? I had to go refill my water. Can anybody get me filled in on what just happened? And then finally, if Zoom becomes disconnected or anything funky happens in the workshop, don't leave the meeting. Stay here. Just let us know in the chat that the video has dropped. Anything that you need us to know, let us know in the chat and we will get it fixed. Paranto. So just stay in the meeting if anything malfunctions. There are resources today for the workshop. We are going to be linking them in the chat section regularly so you don't miss out on any of the material. And as always, guys, this workshop is being recorded and will be inside the lab platform for you to go back to and review in the future. I think that's all I've got. I'm excited to get out of the way and introduce our speaker for today. So happy to have him here live in our studio at Digital Marketer, Donald Miller. Don, how are you today? I'm terrific. Thanks for having me, Bethany. I'm so excited to have you here and I can't wait to dive in to our first session. Yeah. Well, let's get started. Listen, if you don't have the notebook printed out, if you haven't printed this out, you are going to want to do that because it's heavy in content. And a lot of the stuff I'll be referencing will be in this notebook. But if you've signed up for this workshop because you're wondering how in the world do I talk about what I do? How do I talk about what I sell in such a way that people are instantly captivated? They instantly want to know more or maybe even they instantly want to place an order. You're in the right place. They're traditionally in business been three P's to doing business. The first thing we typically do is figure out our product, right? The first P is product. We want that product to be in demand. We want that product to be profitable. Most entrepreneurs, that's where they start. They get that product going and then they realize people want it and so they actually have to hire people in order to sell that product. You didn't realize when you went into business that you basically need a counselor's degree in order to run a business, but you do. It grows and then you've got people around. But once you figure out people and you've got a great culture and everybody seems to be happy and taken care of, then you realize the company is growing even more, we have to figure out processes. And so processes to create the product, processes to market the product, processes to scale. And that's the third P. That's where most people actually stop. And I think your business gets capped. I think the fourth P that we want to talk about today is the key to growing your business further than that. And in my opinion, it's even the first thing you do before you create your product. It's okay to get them out of order if you already have your product and all that, but you don't want to miss the fourth P. It's all we're going to talk about. That is positioning. How is your product actually positioned in the marketplace so that you're differentiated, so that you stand out, so that people immediately know what you offer and know why it matters? You want people to be able to answer very, very quickly what problem do you solve? In fact, I would say if you emailed your customers, let's say you emailed your entire database and you said, what problem do you think we solve? If 70% of the people don't hit reply and answer in the exact same way, you're losing money. In other words, if you haven't taught people to memorize the problem that you solve, you're losing money. And the sad thing is, you will be passed up in the marketplace by a company that has a product that is inferior to yours. Literally, it's not as good of a product, but if they position themselves better, if their customers can answer, this is the problem they solve, they will beat you in the marketplace every single time. We have to communicate extremely clearly about what we offer. Well, all of this story brand and helping people clarify their message and helping people scale their businesses came to me really by accident. I spent 20 years in Portland, Oregon, grew up in Texas, spent 20 years in Portland, Oregon as a writer. I wrote a bunch of memoirs and those did really, really well. I spent a lot of time on best sellers list. But the key thing about writing a memoir or writing books in general or just being a writer is you just sit around in your underwear and you write books and then later people sit around in their underwear and read them and nobody ever interacts in the same room. It was perfect life for me as an introvert. But my assistant came to me and said, Don, I get lots of emails, people are asking questions about your books and they want to talk to you. And she said, can I put together a conference and you would stand on stage and you would present stuff from your books and answer questions. I think it would be a great thing if we did that. I said, go for it. I think it could be fun. We rented a theater in Portland that has about 700 seats and sent an email to our tiny little email list. Again, I wasn't a business guy, I was a writer, I didn't know how to do this stuff. And 350 people registered and showed up. So I'm looking at a room of half empty seats and as a high D on the disc and Enneagram 3, I mean it bothered me. The room was definitely half empty, not half full and I knew we needed to fill it. The other thing is I realized I loved teaching. I didn't realize I was wired as a teacher. I just loved doing it. More than even just speaking, I loved spending all day teaching content and asking questions. So I wanted the business to grow. I was exactly where you are right now. I found something I love and I wanted it to grow. So how do I do that? Well, we got the surveys back from the conference and the surveys were very high. People said we will tell our friends about this and I thought problem solved. And the next time we did the conference, I knew it would be full. We did the conference again however many months later and 350 people showed up. There were 350 empty seats. So in the back of my head is this question. How do you fill up? I had sold millions of books. How do you put more than 350 people in a room? I was in Portland there and I was scheduled to speak at a giant conference in Indianapolis, Indiana. And I got on an airplane early that morning to speak in Indiana that night. And sitting next to me on the airplane, this had never happened to me before, sitting next to me was somebody reading my book. And I had never seen any of my books out in the wild. I didn't know what to do. What would you do if you sat down and the guy had your book? I thought maybe I could read it to him. It will be like the audio book. I said, you know, jokingly, do you like that book? Now my picture is on the inside flap of the book. And he looked at me and he said, I love this book. I have read this book three times. In fact, I am flying to Indianapolis tonight to hear this guy speak. He is my favorite author. And I thought, well, he is going to get it in a minute, right? Because he will figure it out and he didn't. And I realized, wait a second, I have got a couple hours with a customer that I can get honest feedback from. So he will get it soon enough. He never did. For two hours he didn't get it. And I just asked him a lot of questions. In fact, I never even told him who I was. I figured he is going to figure it out tonight when I walk on stage. He never came up to me. So if you ever meet that guy, tell him I am really sorry. But it was great market research, right? And so I am asking him, what do you like about this guy? Would you ever attend a conference? Why do you want to hear him speak? All that kind of stuff. And what I kept hearing was the same thing that I think a lot of you guys are hearing about your business and about your products. You are hearing things like, well, you have to try to understand. He was saying you have to read his books to really understand them, those kinds of things. The more he talked about my books, to be brutally honest, the less I wanted to buy them. In fact, I am not even sure I wanted to hear myself speak that night. It just sounded so boring. What he didn't have were sound bites that would make me interested in buying my own products. And then I realized later, I never gave those folks sound bites. I never gave them little bits of information they could pass along to friends that would make them want to buy my product or service. So that is on me. What I discovered is what some of you have discovered. It is I am really good at writing 300 pages and I am not good at writing the paragraph on the back of the book that makes you want to buy it. But we have to realize if our product is really good, that does not ensure success. What ensures success is a product that is really good and some sound bites that make people want to buy the product. We have to do both. And so what we are going to do today is we are going to explore a 2,000 year old formula. It is not mine. He really knows who it is or where it came from. The first guy who wrote it down was a gentleman named Aristotle who wrote a book called Poetics. We are going to explore story and we are going to ask why does story have so much power to get people's attention and how can we leverage the power of story and create sound bites that invite people into a story that would make them want to buy our products and services. That is what we are going to do today. I created the framework and I began to test it in my own conference business and immediately we sold out the 700 room auditorium. We went to a 1500 seat auditorium and sold that out. Went to a 2500 seat auditorium and sold that out. I realized the framework itself, the marketing framework may be more valuable than the actual thing I am selling. I switched gears and wrote a book called Building a Story Brand and have now helped somewhere in the area of about 50,000 businesses figure out their marketing message. It really, really matters. It is going to be the key. When you start talking about your business in such a way that people begin to understand it easily and quickly, you will find that you sell more products. All that being said, show me jazz hands if you feel like you are in the right room and you signed up for the right workshop. All right, I am seeing some jazz hands out there and that is encouraging.
